components.name: build_metrics.domain
components.responsibility: Define BuildRun entity with invariants. Immutable dataclass with id, start_time, end_time, exit_status fields. Provides duration_seconds property and is_success() method. Validates end_time >= start_time.

components.name: build_metrics.persistence
components.responsibility: JSONL-based repository for BuildRun objects. Owns storage format (JSONL at ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl). Provides save() for append-only writes and get_last_n() for retrieval with malformed line recovery and 10MB file size warnings.

components.name: build_metrics.service
components.responsibility: Compute aggregate statistics over BuildRun collections. MetricsService provides compute_stats() method that calculates average duration and success rate. Returns StatsResult dataclass with avg_duration_seconds and success_rate fields.

components.name: build_metrics.__init__
components.responsibility: Export public API from bounded context. Re-exports BuildRun, BuildMetricsRepository, MetricsService, and StatsResult for external consumers.

components.name: cli.record_command
components.responsibility: CLI handler for 'buildstats record' subcommand. Parses --id, --start-time, --end-time, --exit-status arguments, constructs BuildRun, persists via repository, handles errors gracefully.

components.name: cli.stats_command
components.responsibility: CLI handler for 'buildstats stats' subcommand. Accepts --last-n argument (default 10), invokes MetricsService.compute_stats(), outputs JSON to stdout with avg_duration_seconds and success_rate.

components.name: tests.build_metrics.test_domain
components.responsibility: Unit tests for domain layer. Covers BuildRun instantiation, duration_seconds property, is_success() method, invariant validation (end_time >= start_time), and immutability.

components.name: tests.build_metrics.test_persistence
components.responsibility: Unit tests for persistence layer. Covers save() file creation, JSONL append behavior, get_last_n() retrieval, malformed JSON handling, empty storage edge cases, and n <= 0 behavior.

components.name: tests.build_metrics.test_service
components.responsibility: Unit tests for service layer. Covers compute_stats() accuracy for average duration, success rate calculation, respecting last_n parameter, and empty storage handling.

components.name: tests.build_metrics.test_cli_integration
components.responsibility: End-to-end integration tests for CLI commands. Covers 'record' persistence, 'stats' JSON output, empty storage handling, and time validation errors. Uses isolated HOME directory fixture.

decisions.decision: Use @dataclass instead of TypedDict for StatsResult
decisions.rationale: TypedDict is a static type hint only and doesn't provide a runtime constructor. Using @dataclass(frozen=True) provides both static typing AND runtime instantiation, fixing the critical bug where StatsResult(avg_duration_seconds=0.0, success_rate=0.0) would fail with AttributeError.

PRD explicitly scopes to file-based JSONL. JSONL is human-readable for debugging, append-only simple implementation, and requires zero dependencies. For ~10k runs (<10MB), performance is acceptable. Future migration to DB requires rewriting BuildMetricsRepository only (interface unchanged).

Reading entire file and slicing last N lines is simple and reliable vs. complex reverse seek with variable-length lines. Performance warning at 10MB guides users before latency becomes problematic. Memory usage = O(file size), acceptable at 10MB.

decisions.decision: Explicit type hints on all interfaces including argparse.Namespace
decisions.rationale: Tech lead feedback identified missing type hints. For autonomous agent execution, precision eliminates ambiguity. Every callable now has complete PEP 484 annotations so agents can copy signatures verbatim without type inference guessing.

PRD Risk #2 requires resilience to corruption. Raising exceptions would fail entire query and lose all data after first corruption. Skipping bad lines allows partial results while warning to stderr notifies user of data integrity issues.

Tech lead required this (Issue #2). 10MB ≈ 50k runs (200 bytes/run). Reading entire file takes ~100ms (SSD) at this scale. Warning guides users before latency becomes problematic (1GB = 10s read). No automated mitigation as archiving is out-of-scope.

Follows existing codebase pattern where all modules live directly under src/buildstats/. Each layer (domain, persistence, service) gets its own file to enable parallel agent execution without merge conflicts.

decisions.decision: argparse subcommands for record and stats
decisions.rationale: Extends existing CLI pattern using add_subparsers(dest='command'). Consistent with existing 'run' subcommand. Keeps all CLI logic in single cli.py file with private _record_command() and _stats_command() handlers.

architecture.file_changes_overview: Creates new bounded context module src/buildstats/build_metrics/ with 4 files (domain.py, persistence.py, service.py, __init__.py). Modifies existing src/buildstats/cli.py to add record and stats subcommands with handlers. Creates comprehensive test suite in tests/build_metrics/ with 4 test modules (test_domain.py, test_persistence.py, test_service.py, test_cli_integration.py) plus __init__.py. Total: 9 new files, 1 modified file. All components file-isolated for parallel execution.
interfaces: BuildRun.__init__(id: str, start_time: datetime, end_time: datetime, exit_status: int)

aggregates.name: BuildRun
aggregates.responsibility: Immutable record of a single build run with timing and outcome

domain_services.name: MetricsService
domain_services.responsibility: Compute aggregate statistics (avg duration, success rate) over collections of BuildRun objects

bounded_contexts.name: build_metrics
bounded_contexts.purpose: Persist build run data (id, start_time, end_time, exit_status) to disk and compute aggregate statistics (average duration, success rate) over last N runs.

guardrails.enforcement: Manual review. Every function signature includes parameter types and return type.
guardrails.rule: All modules must have complete type hints (PEP 484)

guardrails.enforcement: Import analysis. domain.py imports only from stdlib (dataclasses, datetime).
guardrails.rule: Domain layer has zero external dependencies

guardrails.enforcement: File boundary = isolation boundary. Module dependency graph enforces layer separation.
guardrails.rule: Each component owns distinct files (no merge conflicts)

guardrails.enforcement: get_last_n() checks file size and logs to stderr if > 10MB.
try/except in get_last_n() catches JSON errors, prints to stderr, continues processing.
guardrails.rule: Malformed JSONL lines are logged and skipped

guardrails.enforcement: compute_stats() checks if runs list is empty and returns StatsResult(0.0, 0.0).

module_contracts.notes: Zero dependencies. Pure domain model with invariant validation.
provides: BuildRun dataclass with id, start_time, end_time, exit_status

module_contracts.notes: Owns physical storage format and location. Handles malformed JSONL gracefully.
Business logic for analytics queries. Returns zeros for empty datasets.
provides: StatsResult dataclass with avg_duration_seconds and success_rate

module_contracts.notes: Wires user input to domain/service layers. Handles errors gracefully with exit codes.

observability.detail: Log to stderr when metrics.jsonl exceeds 10MB to warn users of potential performance degradation.
observability.name: File size warnings

observability.detail: Log to stderr when JSONL lines are malformed, including line number and error details.
observability.name: Malformed data warnings

observability.detail: All CLI errors (ValueError, OSError, etc.) are caught and printed to stderr with descriptive messages before returning exit code 1.
observability.name: CLI error messages

vertical_slice.description: End-to-end flow: User runs 'buildstats record --id X --start-time T1 --end-time T2 --exit-status 0' → CLI parses args → BuildRun created with invariant validation → Repository saves to JSONL → File created at ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl. Then 'buildstats stats --last-n 10' → CLI parses args → Service fetches last 10 runs → Computes avg duration and success rate → Returns JSON to stdout. This slice proves the entire persistence and query pipeline.

architecture.summary: This architecture adds a build_metrics bounded context to the buildstats CLI with domain-driven persistence of build run data. The design uses a clean layered architecture: domain layer (BuildRun dataclass with invariants), persistence layer (JSONL file storage), service layer (aggregate statistics computation), and CLI integration (record/stats subcommands). All components are file-isolated for parallel agent execution. Tech lead feedback resolved: TypedDict replaced with @dataclass for runtime construction, file size warning added, type hints completed, docstrings corrected.

acceptance_criteria: Given valid timestamps with end >= start, when BuildRun is instantiated, then it stores all four fields (id, start_time, end_time, exit_status)
Given a BuildRun with start_time and end_time, when duration_seconds property is accessed, then it returns (end_time - start_time).total_seconds()
Given a BuildRun with exit_status=0, when is_success() is called, then it returns True
Given a BuildRun with exit_status != 0, when is_success() is called, then it returns False
Given timestamps where end_time < start_time, when BuildRun is instantiated, then it raises ValueError with message containing 'end_time' and 'start_time'
Given a BuildRun instance, when attempting to assign a new value to any field, then it raises an error (frozen dataclass)
issues.description: Delivers the BuildRun frozen dataclass that represents a single build run record. Enforces the core invariant that end_time >= start_time and provides duration computation. This is the foundation entity consumed by all other layers.
guardrails: Frozen dataclass prevents mutation after construction

guidance.review_focus: Verify __post_init__ raises ValueError for invalid time order. Confirm dataclass is frozen. Check that duration_seconds handles subsecond precision correctly.
guidance.risk_rationale: Foundation entity with simple invariants. Straightforward dataclass implementation with well-defined validation.
guidance.testing_guidance: Unit tests for each dataclass behavior (instantiation, properties, methods, invariants). Test both success and failure paths for validation. Verify immutability with frozen dataclass.

issues.name: domain-model
provides: BuildRun dataclass with fields: id, start_time, end_time, exit_status
issues.testing_strategy: Create tests/build_metrics/test_domain.py using pytest. Red→Green→Refactor, one behavior at a time, simplest first. 🔴 valid instantiation (AC1): write test_buildrun_creation that fails because domain.py doesn't exist. 🟢 create domain.py with minimal BuildRun dataclass (4 fields, frozen=True). 🔴 duration_seconds property (AC2) → 🟢 add @property. 🔴 is_success for exit_status=0 (AC3) → 🔴 is_success for exit_status≠0 (AC4) → 🟢 implement is_success() method. 🔴 time invariant violation (AC5) → 🟢 add __post_init__ validation. 🔴 immutability test (AC6) → already passes with frozen=True. 🔵 Refactor: extract validation logic if needed, add comprehensive docstrings. Covers AC1–AC6.
issues.title: Create BuildRun domain entity with time invariants

verification.command: python -c "from buildstats.build_metrics.domain import BuildRun; import datetime; br = BuildRun(id='abc', start_time=datetime.datetime.now(), end_time=datetime.datetime.now(), exit_status=0); assert br.id == 'abc' and br.exit_status == 0" && echo PASS

acceptance_criteria: Given a new BuildMetricsRepository with no storage_path override, when instantiated, then it uses ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl as the default path
Given a BuildRun object, when save() is called, then it creates ~/.buildstats/ directory if missing and appends a JSON line with keys: id, start_time (ISO8601), end_time (ISO8601), exit_status
Given 5 persisted runs, when get_last_n(3) is called, then it returns a list of 3 BuildRun objects representing the last 3 lines from JSONL
Given a non-existent storage file, when get_last_n(10) is called, then it returns an empty list without raising an error
Given get_last_n(0) or get_last_n(-5), when called, then it returns an empty list
Given a JSONL file with valid line, invalid line, valid line, when get_last_n(10) is called, then it returns 2 BuildRun objects and logs a warning to stderr for the malformed line
Given a storage file > 10MB, when get_last_n() is called, then it logs a performance warning to stderr mentioning file size and archiving
Given two save() calls, when inspecting the JSONL file, then it contains exactly two lines, each a valid JSON object, ordered chronologically
issues.description: Delivers the BuildMetricsRepository that serializes BuildRun objects to append-only JSONL storage at ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl. Handles deserialization, malformed-line recovery, and file-size warnings. This is the data gateway for all build metrics.
guardrails: Malformed JSONL lines logged to stderr and skipped
Storage directory created automatically with mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

guidance.review_focus: Verify malformed JSONL lines are skipped with stderr warnings (not exceptions). Confirm file size warning triggers at >10MB. Check that _ensure_storage_dir uses mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True). Ensure get_last_n returns most-recent-last ordering.
guidance.risk_rationale: Core I/O layer with error recovery. Well-defined error handling strategy (log-and-skip). File operations are standard library, low risk.
guidance.testing_guidance: Unit tests with tmp_path fixtures for file I/O isolation. Test save/load round-trip, edge cases (empty file, n<=0, malformed JSON), and warnings (use capsys to capture stderr). Property test for save/get_last_n round-trip invariant.

issues.name: persistence-layer
observability: Log malformed JSONL lines to stderr with line number
provides: BuildMetricsRepository class with __init__(storage_path: Path | None = None)
issues.testing_strategy: Create tests/build_metrics/test_persistence.py using pytest with tmp_path fixtures. Red→Green→Refactor, simplest behaviors first. 🔴 default storage path (AC1) → 🟢 minimal __init__ with Path.home(). 🔴 save creates file and directory (AC2) → 🟢 implement save() with _ensure_storage_dir(). 🔴 save appends valid JSONL (AC8) → 🟢 add JSON serialization with isoformat(). 🔴 get_last_n with empty file (AC4) → 🟢 implement exists() check. 🔴 get_last_n(0) and negative (AC5) → 🟢 add n <= 0 guard. 🔴 get_last_n returns correct count and order (AC3) → 🟢 implement _read_last_n_lines and deserialization. 🔴 malformed JSON handling (AC6) → 🟢 add try/except with stderr logging (use capsys fixture). 🔴 file size warning (AC7) → 🟢 add stat().st_size check with stderr warning. 🔵 Refactor: extract _read_last_n_lines helper, consolidate error messages. Property test (Hypothesis): for any list of BuildRuns, saving then retrieving preserves all fields. Covers AC1–AC8.
issues.title: Implement JSONL repository for BuildRun persistence

verification.command: python -c "from buildstats.build_metrics.persistence import BuildMetricsRepository; BuildMetricsRepository()" && echo PASS

acceptance_criteria: Given an empty repository (0 runs), when compute_stats(10) is called, then it returns StatsResult(avg_duration_seconds=0.0, success_rate=0.0)
Given 3 runs with durations 60s, 120s, 180s (all exit_status=0), when compute_stats(3) is called, then it returns StatsResult with avg_duration_seconds within 0.01 of 120.0
Given 4 runs with exit_status [0, 1, 0, 1], when compute_stats(4) is called, then it returns StatsResult with success_rate within 0.01 of 0.5
Given 10 runs (first 5 failures, last 5 successes), when compute_stats(5) is called, then it returns StatsResult with success_rate=1.0 (only analyzes last 5)
Given compute_stats(0) or compute_stats(-5), when called, then it returns StatsResult(avg_duration_seconds=0.0, success_rate=0.0)
issues.description: Delivers MetricsService that computes average duration and success rate over the last N BuildRun records. Handles empty-set edge cases (returns zeros) and provides the StatsResult read model for downstream consumption.

guidance.review_focus: Verify empty-list check returns StatsResult(0.0, 0.0). Confirm division-by-zero is prevented. Check that success_rate uses run.is_success() method from domain layer.
guidance.risk_rationale: Pure computation layer with no I/O. Simple arithmetic over collections. Well-defined edge cases.
guidance.testing_guidance: Unit tests with mock/temp repository. Test empty-set behavior, average/rate computations, and windowing. Property test for invariants (success_rate in [0,1], avg_duration >= 0).

issues.name: service-layer
provides: StatsResult dataclass with avg_duration_seconds and success_rate fields
issues.testing_strategy: Create tests/build_metrics/test_service.py using pytest with temp_repo fixture (BuildMetricsRepository backed by tmp_path). Red→Green→Refactor, simplest behaviors first. 🔴 empty storage returns zeros (AC1) → 🟢 minimal compute_stats with empty-list guard returning StatsResult(0.0, 0.0). 🔴 avg_duration with 3 runs (AC2) → 🟢 implement sum(run.duration_seconds)/len(runs). 🔴 success_rate with mixed statuses (AC3) → 🟢 implement sum(run.is_success())/len(runs). 🔴 respects last_n windowing (AC4) → already works via repository.get_last_n(). 🔴 n<=0 returns zeros (AC5) → already works via repository.get_last_n() contract. 🔵 Refactor: extract any duplicated summing logic. Property test (Hypothesis): for any list of runs, 0.0 <= success_rate <= 1.0 and avg_duration >= 0.0. Covers AC1–AC5.
issues.title: Implement aggregate statistics computation service

verification.command: python -c "from buildstats.build_metrics.persistence import BuildMetricsRepository; from buildstats.build_metrics.service import MetricsService; import tempfile; from pathlib import Path; repo = BuildMetricsRepository(Path(tempfile.mktemp())); svc = MetricsService(repo); print('PASS')"

acceptance_criteria: Given 'buildstats record --help', when executed, then the output includes the '--id' flag description
Given 'buildstats stats --help', when executed, then the output includes the '--last-n' flag description

Given 'buildstats record --id test1 --start-time 2026-06-26T10:00:00 --end-time 2026-06-26T10:05:00 --exit-status 0', when executed, then it creates ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl and returns exit code 0
Given a persisted run with id='test1', when 'buildstats record' retrieves it via repository.get_last_n(1), then the returned BuildRun has id='test1'
Given 'buildstats stats --last-n 5' with no persisted runs, when executed, then it prints JSON with avg_duration_seconds=0 and success_rate=0 and returns exit code 0
Given 'buildstats stats --last-n 5' with 3 persisted runs, when executed, then it prints valid JSON to stdout containing 'avg_duration_seconds' and 'success_rate' keys
Given 'buildstats record' with end_time < start_time, when executed, then it prints an error to stderr and returns exit code 1
Given 'buildstats stats' with no --last-n flag, when executed, then it defaults to last_n=10
issues.description: Extends the existing buildstats CLI with two new subcommands: 'record' (persists a BuildRun) and 'stats' (queries aggregate statistics). Integrates domain, persistence, and service layers into the argparse-based CLI entrypoint. This is the vertical slice that exposes the full feature to end users.
guardrails: ValueError and OSError caught in _record_command, logged to stderr
Exception caught in _stats_command, logged to stderr

guidance.review_focus: Verify _record_command catches ValueError and OSError, logs to stderr, returns exit code 1. Confirm _stats_command outputs single-line JSON (no pretty-printing). Check that imports are added correctly at top of cli.py. Ensure main() dispatch includes new elif branches.
guidance.risk_rationale: Integration point wiring multiple layers. Higher complexity due to argparse setup, error handling, and JSON output formatting. Needs deeper QA to verify end-to-end contracts.
guidance.testing_guidance: Integration tests using monkeypatch for HOME isolation. Test both subcommands end-to-end with real file I/O (temp directories). Verify help text, exit codes, JSON output format, and error handling. Use capsys to capture stdout/stderr.

issues.name: cli-integration

provides: CLI dispatch logic routing to record/stats handlers
JSON output to stdout for stats command
issues.testing_strategy: Create tests/build_metrics/test_cli_integration.py using pytest with monkeypatch to isolate HOME directory (prevents polluting real ~/.buildstats). Red→Green→Refactor, CLI behaviors simplest first. 🔴 record --help shows --id (AC1) → 🟢 add record subparser with add_argument('--id'). 🔴 stats --help shows --last-n (AC2) → 🟢 add stats subparser with add_argument('--last-n', default=10). 🔴 record creates file and returns 0 (AC3) → 🟢 implement _record_command with BuildRun instantiation and repository.save(). 🔴 record persists data retrievable via repo (AC4) → already works from AC3. 🔴 stats with empty storage returns zeros (AC5) → 🟢 implement _stats_command with service.compute_stats() and json.dumps(). 🔴 stats with data returns JSON (AC6) → already works from AC5. 🔴 record with invalid time order returns error (AC7) → 🟢 add try/except in _record_command catching ValueError. 🔴 stats defaults to last_n=10 (AC8) → already set in add_argument default. 🔵 Refactor: update __init__.py exports, ensure imports are at top of cli.py. Covers AC1–AC8.

issues.description: Executes all PRD-level acceptance criteria as integration tests to confirm the build-metrics bounded context delivers the specified contracts. This is the final verification gate before completion.

guidance.review_focus: Verify each test executes the EXACT command from PRD acceptance criteria. Confirm tests use isolated temp directories (no ~/.buildstats pollution). Check that test names map 1:1 to PRD AC numbers.
guidance.risk_rationale: Verification-only issue. No implementation—just test scaffolding. Low risk, confirms feature completeness.
guidance.testing_guidance: Black-box integration tests executing CLI commands via subprocess. Use monkeypatch for HOME isolation. Test exact PRD acceptance criteria commands.

issues.name: end-to-end-verification
issues.testing_strategy: Create tests/build_metrics/test_prd_acceptance.py using pytest with subprocess to execute actual CLI commands (not just Python function calls). Red→Green sequence: 🔴 For each PRD AC (AC1-AC11), write a test that executes the exact shell command from the PRD and asserts exit code 0. 🟢 Tests pass once all prior issues are implemented. 🔵 Refactor: extract common setup (temp HOME directory) into fixtures. These are black-box integration tests from the user's perspective—no internal API knowledge assumed. Covers all 11 PRD acceptance criteria.
issues.title: Verify full feature against PRD acceptance criteria

invariants: id must be non-empty string (identity requirement)
aggregates.name: BuildRun
aggregates.responsibility: Immutable record of a single build execution with temporal and outcome data

domain_events.name: BuildRunRecorded
domain_events.payload_summary: BuildRun aggregate state (id, start_time, end_time, exit_status, duration_seconds)

domain_events.name: StatsComputed
domain_events.payload_summary: Aggregate statistics result (avg_duration_seconds, success_rate, sample_size, timestamp)

domain_events.name: StorageCorruptionDetected
domain_events.payload_summary: Malformed JSONL line details (line_number, error_type, raw_content)

domain_services.name: MetricsService
domain_services.responsibility: Compute aggregate statistics (average duration, success rate) over collections of BuildRun aggregates

domain_services.name: BuildMetricsRepository
domain_services.responsibility: Persist and retrieve BuildRun aggregates using JSONL storage, enforcing append-only semantics

bounded_contexts.name: build_metrics
bounded_contexts.purpose: Persist and analyze build run data to provide aggregate statistics on build performance and reliability

current_diagram.title: Current State - BuildStats CLI (Pre-Metrics)

bus.description: Synchronous in-process event bus for v1. Events are published to a simple observer list. Handlers execute within the same call stack as the publisher. No persistence, no retry, no ordering guarantees beyond synchronous execution.
bus.migration_notes: Future migration to async/queue-based transport justified ONLY if: (1) CLI latency becomes unacceptable due to event handler overhead, (2) need to decouple write path from event processing, or (3) introducing cross-context event consumers. Current design assumes single-context, single-process execution.

extraction_strategy.description: Extraction of build_metrics bounded context into standalone library gated on: (1) vertical slice functional test passing (record + stats round-trip), (2) all unit tests passing (domain, persistence, service layers), (3) integration test confirming event emission for BuildRunRecorded and StatsComputed. Extraction steps: (1) create separate repo buildstats-metrics-lib, (2) move src/buildstats/build_metrics/ to lib root, (3) extract event bus interface to abstract base class (currently implicit), (4) publish as pip package, (5) update buildstats CLI to depend on buildstats-metrics-lib, (6) maintain JSONL storage contract for backward compatibility. Extraction enables: reuse of build_metrics in other tools (CI dashboards, notification services), independent versioning of domain vs presentation layers, clearer bounded context isolation. Do NOT extract until slice is tested and functional - premature extraction risks interface instability.

(Read Model)"] end subgraph "Event Stream" E1["BuildRunRecorded
(Domain Event)"] E2["StatsComputed
(Domain Event)"] end end subgraph "Event Backbone" BUS["In-Process Event Bus
(synchronous)"] end CLI --> RECORD CLI --> STATS CLI --> RUN RECORD --> BR RECORD --> REPO STATS --> SVC BR -."validates".-> INV REPO --> JSONL SVC --> REPO SVC --> STATS_MODEL REPO -."emits".-> E1 SVC -."emits".-> E2 E1 --> BUS E2 --> BUS style CLI fill:#e1f5ff style BR fill:#c8e6c9 style REPO fill:#fff9c4 style SVC fill:#ffccbc style BUS fill:#f3e5f5 style JSONL fill:#ffe0b2
future_diagram.title: Future State - BuildStats with Build-Metrics Context

guardrails.enforcement: Static analysis: grep -r 'from.*persistence\|from.*service' src/buildstats/build_metrics/domain.py must return empty. Enforced via pre-commit hook or CI check.
guardrails.rule: build_metrics.domain must have zero dependencies on build_metrics.persistence or build_metrics.service

guardrails.enforcement: Runtime assertion in event bus: assert event.metadata['producer_context'] in ['build_metrics']. Enforced at event publication time.
guardrails.rule: All domain events must include metadata.producer_context matching the emitting bounded context

guardrails.enforcement: Import linter rule: cli.py may only import from build_metrics.__init__ public API, not from build_metrics.domain directly. Enforced via pylint custom checker.
guardrails.rule: CLI layer must not import domain logic directly (use service/repository facades only)

guardrails.enforcement: Code review checklist: every repository.save() must be followed by event_bus.publish(BuildRunRecorded). Enforced via integration test asserting event emission.
guardrails.rule: All persistence operations must emit domain events for observability

guardrails.enforcement: Test coverage requirement: every aggregate invariant must have a corresponding test_*_rejects_invalid_* test case. Enforced via pytest marker @pytest.mark.invariant_test.
guardrails.rule: Aggregates must validate invariants in __post_init__ (fail-fast principle)

guardrails.enforcement: Architectural documentation: StatsResult sources explicitly listed. Enforced via code review and architecture decision records (ADRs).
guardrails.rule: Read models must be derived from domain events only, never from direct database queries

payload_fields.name: data
payload_fields.type: dict (event-specific payload: BuildRun fields, StatsResult fields, or error details)

module_contracts.notes: Zero dependencies within build_metrics. Pure domain logic with stdlib only (datetime, dataclasses).
provides: BuildRun.__init__(id: str, start_time: datetime, end_time: datetime, exit_status: int) -> BuildRun

module_contracts.notes: Emits BuildRunRecorded on save(), StorageCorruptionDetected on malformed JSONL, StorageThresholdExceeded when file >10MB. Storage contract: JSONL at ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl with schema {id, start_time (ISO8601), end_time (ISO8601), exit_status}.
Emits StatsComputed event after successful computation. Returns StatsResult read model (CQRS-lite projection from BuildRunRecorded events).
Anti-corruption layer translating CLI args to domain operations. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=error. JSON output to stdout for stats command.

observability.detail: All domain events (BuildRunRecorded, StatsComputed, StorageCorruptionDetected, StorageThresholdExceeded) logged to structured JSON log stream with metadata (event_id, timestamp, correlation_id). Log level: INFO for business events, WARNING for operational events (corruption/threshold).
observability.name: Domain Event Logging

observability.detail: Every CLI command invocation generates a correlation_id (UUID4) passed through to domain events. Enables end-to-end tracing of: user_command -> domain_operation -> event_emission. Logged at command entry/exit with duration_ms.
observability.name: CLI Command Tracing

observability.detail: Instrument BuildMetricsRepository with: save_count (counter), get_last_n_latency_ms (histogram), file_size_bytes (gauge), malformed_lines_skipped (counter). Exposed as StatsD metrics for external monitoring (optional).
observability.name: Repository Operation Metrics

observability.detail: StorageThresholdExceeded event triggers when metrics.jsonl >10MB. StorageCorruptionDetected event emitted on malformed JSONL with line_number and error_type. Both events logged to stderr AND emitted to event bus for potential alerting.
observability.name: Storage Health Monitoring

observability.detail: Log all ValueError exceptions from BuildRun.__post_init__ with full context: rejected_values, invariant_rule, stack_trace. Log level: ERROR. Enables debugging of invalid data entry attempts.
observability.name: Aggregate Invariant Validation Failures

observability.detail: MetricsService.compute_stats() emits StatsComputed event with: sample_size (number of runs analyzed), computation_duration_ms, result_values (avg_duration, success_rate). Enables performance monitoring of analytics queries.
observability.name: Service Computation Telemetry

read_models.name: StatsResult
read_models.purpose: CQRS-lite projection aggregating BuildRunRecorded events into pre-computed statistics (average duration, success rate). Computed on-demand rather than materialized. Optimized for read path: O(N) computation over N BuildRun aggregates.

vertical_slice.description: End-to-end slice: User executes 'buildstats record --id=slice-001 --start-time=2026-06-26T10:00:00 --end-time=2026-06-26T10:05:00 --exit-status=0' -> CLI creates BuildRun aggregate -> Repository persists to JSONL and emits BuildRunRecorded event -> Event logged to observability stream. Then user executes 'buildstats stats --last-n=1' -> MetricsService queries repository -> Computes StatsResult read model -> Emits StatsComputed event -> Returns JSON to stdout. This slice exercises: aggregate creation with invariant validation, persistence layer save/retrieve, service layer computation, event emission at both write and read paths, and CLI integration for both commands. Success criteria: (1) metrics.jsonl contains valid JSONL entry, (2) stats command returns correct avg_duration=300.0 and success_rate=1.0, (3) both BuildRunRecorded and StatsComputed events logged with correlation_id.

acceptance_criteria: Persistence Layer Exists: `python -c "from buildstats.build_metrics.persistence import BuildMetricsRepository; BuildMetricsRepository()" && echo PASS` exits 0

BuildRun Data Class: `python -c "from buildstats.build_metrics.domain import BuildRun; import datetime; br = BuildRun(id='abc', start_time=datetime.datetime.now(), end_time=datetime.datetime.now(), exit_status=0); assert br.id == 'abc' and br.exit_status == 0" && echo PASS` exits 0
Record Command Exists: `buildstats record --help | grep -q 'id'` exits 0

Record Persists Data: `buildstats record --id test1 --start-time 2026-06-26T10:00:00 --end-time 2026-06-26T10:05:00 --exit-status 0 && python -c "from buildstats.build_metrics.persistence import BuildMetricsRepository; repo = BuildMetricsRepository(); runs = repo.get_last_n(1); assert len(runs) == 1 and runs[0].id == 'test1'" && echo PASS` exits 0
Stats Query Command Exists: `buildstats stats --help | grep -q 'last-n'` exits 0
Stats Returns JSON: `buildstats stats --last-n 5 | python -c "import sys, json; data = json.load(sys.stdin); assert 'avg_duration_seconds' in data and 'success_rate' in data" && echo PASS` exits 0

Avg Duration Accuracy: After recording 3 runs (durations: 60s, 120s, 180s, all success), `buildstats stats --last-n 3 | python -c "import sys, json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert abs(d['avg_duration_seconds'] - 120.0) < 0.01" && echo PASS` exits 0

Success Rate Accuracy: After recording 4 runs (2 exit=0, 2 exit=1), `buildstats stats --last-n 4 | python -c "import sys, json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert abs(d['success_rate'] - 0.5) < 0.01" && echo PASS` exits 0

Empty Stats Handled: With zero persisted runs, `buildstats stats --last-n 10 | python -c "import sys, json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); assert d['avg_duration_seconds'] == 0 and d['success_rate'] == 0" && echo PASS` exits 0
Storage File Created: After first `buildstats record`, `test -f ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl && echo PASS` exits 0
Unit Tests Pass: `python -m pytest tests/build_metrics/ -v` exits 0 with all tests passing
assumptions: Storage Location: `~/.buildstats/` directory is acceptable for all users; create if missing
JSONL Format: Each line is a JSON object with keys matching BuildRun field names (ISO8601 for datetimes)
Exit Status Convention: 0 = success, non-zero = failure (standard Unix convention)
No Partial Runs: All record commands provide complete data (id, start_time, end_time, exit_status)
Timestamp Format: ISO8601 strings for CLI input (e.g., `2026-06-26T10:00:00`), parsed via `datetime.fromisoformat()`
Empty Set Behavior: avg_duration and success_rate both return 0 when N=0 or no runs exist
Last N Ordering: "Last N" means the N most recently recorded runs (last N lines in JSONL)
Single Timezone: All timestamps assumed UTC; no timezone conversion logic required
must_have: Bounded Context Module: Create `src/buildstats/build_metrics/` directory with `__init__.py`, `domain.py`, `persistence.py`, `service.py`

Domain Model (domain.py): `BuildRun` dataclass with fields: `id: str`, `start_time: datetime`, `end_time: datetime`, `exit_status: int`. Include `duration_seconds` property returning `(end_time - start_time).total_seconds()`

Persistence (persistence.py): `BuildMetricsRepository` class with methods: `save(build_run: BuildRun) -> None` (append JSONL to `~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl`) and `get_last_n(n: int) -> list[BuildRun]` (read last N lines from JSONL, deserialize to BuildRun objects)

Service Layer (service.py): `MetricsService` class with method `compute_stats(last_n: int) -> dict` returning `{"avg_duration_seconds": float, "success_rate": float}` where success_rate is proportion of runs where exit_status == 0
CLI Integration: `buildstats record` subcommand with args `--id`, `--start-time` (ISO8601), `--end-time` (ISO8601), `--exit-status` (int); wire to instantiate BuildRun and call repository.save()
CLI Integration: `buildstats stats` subcommand with arg `--last-n` (int, default=10); wire to call service.compute_stats() and print JSON to stdout
Unit Tests: Create `tests/build_metrics/` with `test_domain.py` (test BuildRun dataclass instantiation and duration_seconds property)
Unit Tests: Create `test_persistence.py` (test save/get_last_n with temp directory fixtures)
Unit Tests: Create `test_service.py` (test compute_stats with mock repository data)
Unit Tests: Create `test_cli_integration.py` (test CLI commands end-to-end with temp storage)
nice_to_have: Human-readable stats output (table format) when `--format table` flag is passed
`buildstats clear` command to delete all persisted metrics
Progress indicator during stats computation for > 1000 runs
out_of_scope: Database backends (SQLite, PostgreSQL) - file-based JSONL is sufficient
Concurrency/locking - assume single CLI process at a time
Authentication or multi-user support - storage is per-system user
Build run streaming or real-time dashboard
Historical trend charts or time-series analysis
Filtering stats by date ranges or specific exit codes
Distributed metrics aggregation across multiple machines
Integration with external build systems (CI/CD webhooks, etc.)
risks: Large JSONL File Performance: Reading last N lines from a multi-GB file is slow. Mitigation: Document performance degradation threshold (~10k runs). Out-of-scope for v1, but log a warning if file > 10MB.
Corrupted JSONL Lines: Malformed JSON in metrics.jsonl causes deserialization failure. Mitigation: Wrap json.loads() in try/except, skip invalid lines, log warning to stderr.
Concurrent Writes: Two simultaneous `buildstats record` processes may interleave JSONL writes, corrupting data. Acceptance: Documented limitation. Future work: file locking (fcntl).
Clock Skew: end_time < start_time produces negative duration. Mitigation: Validate in BuildRun.__post_init__; raise ValueError if end_time < start_time.
Disk Full: Write to ~/.buildstats/metrics.jsonl fails if disk is full. Mitigation: Let IOError propagate to CLI; user sees error message. No retry logic.
prd.validated_description: Add a build-metrics bounded context to the buildstats CLI that persists build run data (id, start_time, end_time, exit_status) to disk and exposes a query interface to compute average build duration and success rate over the last N runs.

rationale:
This decomposition maximizes parallelism while respecting architectural dependencies. The critical path is: domain-model → persistence-layer → service-layer → cli-integration (4 sequential levels). module-exports can run in parallel with cli-integration once its dependencies are met. end-to-end-verification is the final gate.

Every issue is shaped for Test-Driven Development: each acceptance criterion maps to a Given/When/Then behavior that becomes a failing test first (Red), then minimal implementation (Green), then refactoring (Refactor). Testing strategies explicitly sequence behaviors simplest-first (empty input → single case → multiple cases → edge cases → errors) and call out the Red→Green→Refactor cycle.

File isolation prevents merge conflicts: domain-model, persistence-layer, service-layer, and module-exports create new files; only cli-integration modifies an existing file (cli.py), and it's scheduled last. Tests are isolated in tests/build_metrics/ and depend on implementation modules.

DDD context fields are populated per requirements: bounded_context='build_metrics' on all issues, contract_refs point to exact architecture.md sections, domain_events/read_models/guardrails/observability capture the semantic layer. cli-integration is marked as the vertical-slice (end-to-end path proving the full aggregate/service/event/read-model chain).

Guidance fields shape downstream agent behavior: most issues are needs_deeper_qa=false (straightforward implementation), except cli-integration which is the complex integration point (needs_deeper_qa=true). Testing guidance is concrete and proportional. Verification commands are targeted (pytest -k domain, not the whole suite) to avoid chasing pre-existing repo failures.

The plan delivers all 11 PRD acceptance criteria with full test coverage, explicit error handling (malformed JSONL, time invariants, disk failures), and observability (stderr warnings for file size and corruption). The architecture's canonical code is referenced but NOT duplicated—agents read architecture.md for implementation details. This decomposition is executable by autonomous coder agents with zero clarifying questions.

review.feedback:
Architecture revision #1 successfully addresses all previous tech lead feedback and is ready for autonomous implementation.

**Requirements Coverage (11/11 AC)**:
- AC1-AC11: Every acceptance criterion maps to a specific component with a clear implementation path. No gaps.
- All Must-Have items from PRD are covered by the architecture.
- Test coverage is comprehensive (domain, persistence, service, CLI integration).

**Interface Precision**:
- All type signatures are complete and precise (BuildRun, BuildMetricsRepository, MetricsService, CLI functions).
- Error cases explicitly specified (ValueError for time validation, JSONDecodeError handling, OSError propagation).
- Edge behaviors documented (n <= 0 returns empty list, missing file returns empty list, empty stats return zeros).
- No ambiguity that would require autonomous agents to guess.

**Internal Consistency**:
- Data flow examples match interface definitions exactly (BuildRun construction, repository.save(), service.compute_stats()).
- Component dependency graph matches actual import statements.
- Error handling table matches code implementation (ValueError propagation, log-and-skip for malformed JSON, CLI exception catching).
- StatsResult dataclass definition is consistent across all references.
- Acceptance criteria mapping table references correct components.

**Complexity Calibration**:
- Appropriately simple: JSONL storage (not database), no premature optimization, single concrete repository class.
- Appropriately rigorous: Proper layer separation, comprehensive error handling, file size warnings, complete type hints.
- No over-engineering: Avoids unnecessary abstractions, caching, async, distributed concerns.
- No under-engineering: Enables future migration paths, handles PRD risks.

**Scope Alignment**:
- Implements exactly what PRD specifies: persistence, avg duration, success rate, CLI commands, JSONL storage, unit tests.
- Correctly excludes Nice-to-Have items (table format, clear command, progress indicator, schema versioning).
- Correctly avoids Out-of-Scope items (databases, concurrency, multi-user, streaming, filtering).
- Extension Points section documents future work without expanding current scope.

**Revision Effectiveness**:
All 6 claimed fixes from previous feedback are verified:
1. StatsResult changed from TypedDict to dataclass with proper justification (lines 293-301, 1028-1035).
2. File size warning implemented in get_last_n() (lines 205-212).
3. Error handling docstring corrected to match log-and-skip behavior (lines 199-200).
4. Type hints added to CLI functions: _record_command and _stats_command (lines 451, 476).
5. Parameter documentation clarified for n <= 0 behavior (line 192).
6. Complete AC mapping table added (lines 1152-1164).

**Minor Observations (not blocking)**:
- The canonical code blocks are copy-paste ready, which is excellent for autonomous implementation.
- Performance budget section provides clear degradation thresholds (20ms typical, warnings at 10MB).
- Test specifications are unusually detailed, reducing implementation ambiguity.

**Autonomous Implementation Readiness**:
An autonomous agent can implement this architecture without clarification questions. Every interface is canonical, every error path is specified, every performance characteristic is documented. Code should integrate on first attempt.

**Recommendation**: APPROVED for implementation.
review.summary: Architecture approved. All 11 acceptance criteria have clear implementation paths. Interfaces are precise with complete type signatures and error handling. No internal contradictions found. Complexity is well-calibrated - simple JSONL storage with proper layer separation. Scope exactly matches PRD with no feature creep. All previous tech lead feedback successfully addressed. Ready for autonomous agent implementation.
